news.headline: Vance Heads to Talks as Strait of Hormuz Re-emerges as Point of Tension
news.summary: Vice President JD Vance said he would prioritize nuclear issues and renewed fighting in Lebanon in talks with an Iranian delegation in Switzerland. Shipping in the Strait of Hormuz faced new disruption after Iran’s military said it was closing the waterway.

news.headline: U.S. and Iranian Officials to Meet for Peace Talks in Switzerland
news.summary: The next phase of talks to end the war in Iran is expected to begin on Sunday amid fighting in Lebanon and renewed confusion over the Strait of Hormuz.

news.headline: The Women Running Businesses Under Taliban Rules
news.summary: With secondary education and most jobs out of reach, thousands of Afghan women have turned to entrepreneurship as the only path to make money and maintain a social life.

news.headline: Luxury Kushner Project Collides With Albanian Discontent
news.summary: Protests in Albania against plans for a luxury tourist site have become a cause célèbre for opponents of President Trump and his family. But the politics are local.

news.headline: Cape Verde Will Count on Vozinha Against Uruguay at the World Cup
news.summary: Cape Verde’s veteran goalkeeper, Vozinha, is keeping the archipelago nation’s hopes outsized after his performance against soccer giant Spain.

news.headline: Mona Khalil, Defender of Sea Turtles, Killed in an Israeli Strike in Lebanon
news.summary: For a quarter century, Ms. Khalil ran a guesthouse and worked to protect endangered sea turtles who every summer lay their eggs on a stretch of beach near Tyre, Lebanon.

news.headline: Chaotic run-up to U.S.-Iran talks signals steep hurdles ahead
news.summary: Neither Washington nor Tehran wants the peace process to collapse completely, but diplomats say they expect a process full of challenges and distrust.

news.headline: Man Is Arrested in Scotland After Rampage Leaves 5 Hurt
news.summary: An advocacy group identified some of the victims as Muslims. Counterterrorism authorities are leading the investigation, but a motive for the attacks is so far unknown.

news.summary: Italy’s right-wing prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, was labeled Europe’s “Trump whisperer,” but the war in Iran turned their friendship into a liability for her.

news.headline: Abelardo De La Espriella, Right-Wing Outsider, Could Be Colombia’s Next President
news.summary: President Trump endorsed Abelardo De La Espriella, the conservative presidential candidate in Sunday’s election who has strong U.S. ties.

news.summary: A South Korean island is reviving a long‑forgotten episode involving a French whaler as a wine festival, in an effort to stay relevant.

news.summary: The governing Labour Party may oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer in favor of the more popular Andy Burnham — who will still face all the same challenges.

news.headline: World Leaders Awaited Iran Deal for Months. Here’s Why They’re Still Wary.
news.summary: President Trump’s interim agreement with Iran raises hopes for the calming of the world economy. But it leaves many details for future negotiations, putting the world on edge.

news.headline: Trump Called Iván Cepeda a ‘Radical Left Marxist.’ Can He Be Colombia’s President?
news.summary: Iván Cepeda was caught off guard by a right-wing outsider and anti-leftist anger. He tells The Times he is not giving up.

news.headline: War-weary Iranians see glimmers of relief as Trump deal takes shape
news.summary: As details emerged of an initial agreement between Washington and Tehran, Iranians voiced muted hopes for peace and prosperity.

news.headline: Charles Kushner, U.S. Ambassador to France, Riles Paris With Trump Diplomacy
news.summary: The U.S. envoy to Paris has emerged as the embodiment of combative diplomacy in the age of President Trump, riling the French establishment notably with his accusations of antisemitism.

news.summary: At least one person was killed and dozens of others were injured in the crash near Bedford, England, the authorities said.

news.summary: Clashes in Lebanon seemed to imperil the U.S.-Iran peace deal, and U.S. and Iranian officials postponed negotiations planned in Switzerland before the agreement.

news.headline: U.S. to End AIDS Funding for South Africa
news.summary: According to an unsigned statement from the State Department, the U.S. will phase out support for H.I.V. prevention and treatment in South Africa.

news.headline: Catherine, Princess of Wales Questions Digital Screens’ Role in Children’s Lives
news.summary: The princess published an essay Friday warning against a childhood “mediated by screens,” days after Britain announced a ban on social media for children under 16.

news.headline: ‘Game Changer’? Too Soon to Tell. But Ukraine Flexed in Striking Moscow.
news.summary: The drone attack that sent plumes of smoke rising over Moscow intensified Ukrainian hopes of bringing the war to Russia.

news.headline: A turning point for Haiti? New security force takes fight to powerful gangs
news.summary: Passengers look out of the back of a brightly-coloured shared pick-up style taxi, known locally in Haiti as a “tap tap” as it makes its way along the Boulevard du 15 Octobre in the east of the capital, Port-au-Prince.

news.headline: Children are dying as US sanctions push Cuba to the brink, warns UN human rights chief
news.summary: Children are dying because doctors cannot access essential medicines, UN human rights chief&nbsp;Volker Türk&nbsp;said in a&nbsp;stark warning&nbsp;on Monday, calling for the immediate lifting of United States sanctions against the Caribbean nation that were causing “widespread harm”

news.headline: Haiti: Harrowing needs must be met with long-term engagement
news.summary: Escalating gang violence across Haiti has pushed displacement to record levels, deepening an already severe humanitarian crisis and leaving nearly 1.5 million people without a stable place to live, according to new figures released Friday by the UN migration agency, IOM.

news.headline: Regional health agency issues measles alert for World Cup
news.summary: As World Cup fever rises in the Americas, countries are urged to strengthen measles surveillance and vaccination amid ongoing outbreaks across the region.&nbsp;

news.headline: Haiti: UN rights office welcomes new judicial units to tackle gang violence and impunity
news.summary: The UN&nbsp;human rights office, OHCHR,&nbsp;on Tuesday welcomed the opening of two specialised judicial units in Haiti, saying the move marked&nbsp;“a significant step toward tackling widespread impunity in the country.”&nbsp;

news.headline: Haitian children ‘paying the highest price’ amid surge in gang recruitment
news.summary: As gangs continue to “terrorise” communities in Haiti, children are the ones paying the highest price, the UN Special Representative for Children and Armed Conflict warned on Thursday.

news.headline: 30,000 people flee fresh violence in Haiti as hunger crisis deepens
news.summary: Around 30,000 people have had to flee their homes in the Haitian capital Port-au-Prince and surrounding areas following a new wave of violent attacks and atrocities perpetrated by armed gangs over the past ten days.

news.headline: Extreme heat, floods and drought threaten lives across Latin America and Caribbean
news.summary: Record-breaking temperatures, deadly floods, worsening drought and intensifying hurricanes are placing millions of people across Latin America and the Caribbean at growing risk of hunger, displacement and water shortages,&nbsp;according to a new report from the World Meteorological Organization

news.headline: Blackouts and shortages disrupt healthcare across Cuba
news.summary: Hospitals across Cuba are suspending surgeries, struggling to keep lifesaving equipment running and facing severe medicine shortages as blackouts and fuel shortages push the country’s healthcare system deeper into crisis, senior UN officials warned on Friday.

news.headline: Gang-controlled streets, shuttered newsrooms: How violence is eroding Haiti’s media
news.summary: Journalists working in Haiti are under constant threat of death or injury from rapidly expanding criminal gangs, as they continue to report news and information which they hope will help keep fellow citizens safe.

news.headline: UN rights chief urges action to end impunity and address disappearances in Mexico
news.summary: Mexican authorities must intensify efforts to end impunity, protect journalists and human rights defenders, and address the country’s “painful” crisis of disappearances, UN human rights chief Volker Türk has said.

news.headline: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence
news.summary: The Security Council is addressing the deepening crisis in Haiti on Thursday, centered on the Secretary-General’s latest report which highlights a security landscape of both intensified enforcement and rising civilian risk. While operations including by the UN-backed&nbsp;Gang Suppression Force

news.headline: As Colombia elections near, Security Council hears calls for calm
news.summary: While hope for peace is alive in Colombia as the country prepares for presidential elections, the Security Council on Tuesday heard that achieving it – and making it last – still requires the full implementation of a peace accord signed almost 10 years ago.

news.headline: More than half of Haitians continue to face food crisis
news.summary: 5.8 million Haitians, or roughly 52 per cent of the population, are facing crisis levels of food insecurity, or worse. Of those, more than 1.8 million are dealing with emergency levels, which means they are exhausting their last assets and unable to meet even basic food needs.

news.headline: Shockwaves of Middle East war reach Caribbean as food prices soar
news.summary: Six weeks since war erupted in the Middle East, the shockwaves have spread to the Caribbean region, already pushed to the brink, amid fears of a looming El Niño-linked climate disaster.

news.headline: The Epstein files: Rights experts demand accountability, call for probe into trafficking allegations
news.summary: UN independent human rights experts&nbsp;called on Thursday for justice and accountability for young women and girls who were trafficked systematically as part of allegations contained in the so-called Epstein files.

news.headline: Tobago’s teen archery champion takes aim at sustainability targets
news.summary: Six-time Trinidad and Tobago archery champion, Anthurium Lewis, has told UN News how sport helped her overcome age barriers in environmental advocacy and how in the future it can contribute to reaching globally agreed poverty and sustainability goals.

news.headline: From conflict to catwalk: Women ex-combatants weave reconciliation in Colombia
news.summary: Katerine Avella is a former combatant in Colombia’s decades-long civil war, a peace signatory and a community leader. After the guns fell silent, she created the fashion brand Ixora but, with violence returning to the region, Ms. Avella is now focusing on trying to keep the project afloat in the fac

news.headline: Haiti’s freefall demands urgent global action as millions face hunger and violence
news.summary: Haiti is facing “one of the most severe and rapidly deteriorating humanitarian crises in the Western Hemisphere,” a senior UN aid official warned on Friday, underscoring the need for continued global attention to alleviate suffering there.&nbsp;

news.headline: Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies
news.summary: The UN has issued an urgent call for international support as Cuba grapples with a ‘worsening’ humanitarian crisis fuelled by a prolonged energy blockade and the lingering devastation caused by&nbsp;Hurricane Melissa last year.
